abstract.t: This document defines the Vanta control plane, acknowledgement model, flow control, retransmission signaling, reconnection semantics, and effectively-once mutation behavior.

section.name: Control Plane and Data Plane
section.t: Vanta distinguishes control-plane traffic from data-plane traffic. Control-plane frames include acknowledgements, flow control, errors, heartbeats, stream management, and audit receipts.
Control-plane traffic SHOULD be prioritized over application data traffic.

section.name: Acknowledgement Model
section.t: Vanta distinguishes transport acknowledgement from semantic acknowledgement.

li.t: Transport acknowledgement confirms receipt and parse success.

section.name: ACK Frame
section.t: ACK frames confirm receipt of one or more frames on a target stream. They include at least a target stream identifier, the largest contiguous received sequence number, and optional selective acknowledgement ranges.

section.name: NACK Frame
section.t: NACK frames indicate missing or rejected frame sequences. A NACK does not by itself require retransmission, but it provides an explicit loss or rejection signal.

section.name: Flow Control
section.t: Flow control is receiver-driven. Receivers grant byte credits and optionally message credits. Senders MUST stop sending data-plane traffic when credits are exhausted.
Implementations SHOULD reserve capacity for control-plane traffic to avoid deadlock.

section.name: Heartbeat
section.t: HEARTBEAT frames are used for liveness detection and optional round-trip time measurement.

section.name: Retransmission
section.t: Retransmission is explicit. Retransmitted frames MUST preserve MessageID and any relevant OperationID. Retransmitted frames SHOULD set the RETRANSMIT flag.

section.name: Effectively-Once Mutation Semantics
section.t: Vanta targets effectively-once semantics for mutation requests. Duplicate detection is based on sender identity and mutation identifiers rather than transport guarantees alone.
Receivers SHOULD maintain an in-memory deduplication cache for the active session lifetime.

section.name: Reconnection and Failure Semantics
section.t: Reconnection uses policy-defined backoff and optional resumption support. Unresolved mutations MUST be treated as UNKNOWN_AFTER_DISCONNECT until explicitly reconciled.

section.name: Ordering
section.t: Ordering may be negotiated or requested per stream. The default SHOULD favor throughput and low latency unless stronger ordering semantics are explicitly requested.

section.name: Error Handling
section.t: ERROR frames provide explicit and structured protocol failures, including authentication errors, schema violations, flow-control failures, and routing or capability errors.
